title: rubus coombensis
intro: Rubus coombensis, also known as Coombe's blackberry, is a species of flowering plant in the rose family. It is native to New Zealand, where its fruit is edible, rich in antioxidants and has been traditionally used for medicinal purposes. The plant is valued for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate digestive issues, reduce fever, and support wound healing. Rubus coombensis can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, making it a versatile herbal remedy for various health benefits.
